cwes.desc: The web application does not, or can not, sufficiently verify whether a well-formed, valid, consistent request was intentionally provided by the user who submitted the request (Cross-Site Request Forgery).

The product does not use or incorrectly uses a protection mechanism that provides sufficient defense against directed attacks against the product (missing security headers such as X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy).
The product does not restrict or incorrectly restricts access to a resource from an unauthorized actor.

The product uses external input to construct a pathname that is intended to identify a file or directory that is located underneath a restricted parent directory, but the product does not properly neutralize special elements within the pathname that can cause the pathname to resolve to a location that is outside of the restricted directory.
The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input before it is placed in output that is used as a web page that is served to other users.
